title: Add --no-creds flag to skopeo inspect
body:
Follow PR #433 
Close #421 
Currently skopeo inspect allows to:

- Use the default credentials in $HOME/.docker.config
- Explicitly define credentials via de --creds flag
This implements a --no-creds flag which will query docker registries anonymously.

Signed-off-by: Qi Wang <qiwan@redhat.com>

comments.body: OK, I might not be remembering correctly, but I thought that `skopeo inspect` worked against a registry that didn't require creds already?  If so, why do we need the `--no-creds` flag?

> why do we need the `--no-creds` flag?

See https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/433#issuecomment-337062649 .
Thanks @mtrmac.  Prior to this patch, could they just `docker/podman logout` and then pull successfully?
to document `c/image/types.DockerAuthConfig` can be used with empty fields, just change the comment in `c/image/types/types.go` ?

> to document `c/image/types.DockerAuthConfig` can be used with empty fields, just change the comment in `c/image/types/types.go` ?

Yes; we just need such a PR to exist in c/image, this PR does not need to block on it nor vendor an updated version of c/image just for the comment.
